items.title: Ocean Census discovers over 1,100 new marine species
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Tasmanian government businesses defy premier's call for public disclosure logs
items.summary: Tasmanian government-owned businesses or companies are being threatened with legislation to force them to disclose right-to-information documents publicly after nine of them failed to respond to the premier's request.

items.title: Man to face court over alleged rape at Mount Pleasant
items.summary: A man who allegedly broke into a home and sexually assaulted the occupant will face court charged with aggravated serious criminal trespass and rape.

items.title: Federal government prepares diphtheria support package after more than 220 cases reported
items.summary: Federal and state governments have mobilised to address the largest outbreak of diphtheria in recent memory, with the Commonwealth preparing a support package for a disease once considered almost eradicated.

items.title: Infant death sparks call for use of church land to tackle homelessness in Wagga Wagga
items.summary: Church land is being touted as a potential solution to the homelessness crisis in a southern NSW city still reeling from the death of a newborn baby in a tent almost three weeks ago.

items.title: Matildas squad named for Mexico friendlies with teen keeper included
items.summary: Mary Fowler, Ellie Carpenter and Steph Catley are back in the Matildas squad for games against Mexico, while a teenager has been called up.

items.title: James Blee, 24, found guilty of helping father with Newcastle cocaine shipment
items.summary: A Queensland man has been found guilty of involvement with his father's attempt to smuggle a massive amount of cocaine into the NSW port city.

items.title: What life is like as an older parent, or being born to one
items.summary: After publishing our story on having babies in your 40s, we heard from readers on what being, or having, an older parent is like.

items.title: AVG Travels fallout deepens as more customers report cancelled or rescheduled tours
items.summary: The ABC has been contacted by more than 100 travellers since first reporting on the matter on Monday, with some claiming their tours were cancelled at the last minute and itineraries changed.

items.title: Plans to revive WA's once-booming nickel plants to cash in on high gold prices
items.summary: Idle infrastructure from WA's once-booming nickel industry is set to be retrofitted to produce gold bars amid historic high prices for the precious metal.

items.title: Israel says no live ammunition used by troops filmed firing at Gaza flotilla boats
items.summary: The Australian government says it is engaging with Israel over the welfare of 11 Australians detained, as Israeli forces fire "non-lethal" shots at two boats in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla.

items.title: NRL confirms Tigers, Eels, Titans and Storm will play 2027 Las Vegas fixtures
items.summary: Jarome Luai will begin his final season with the Wests Tigers in Las Vegas, with the NRL unveiling its teams for next year's season-openers.

items.title: ACT to fund dozens of new emergency services roles after review found 'chronic' ambulance understaffing
items.summary: The ACT government has unveiled a $94.3 million emergency services package, but the union says it still falls short by about 50 staff needed to prevent future station closures.

items.title: Storm Hunter and Tristan Schoolkate advance in French Open qualifiers as seeded stars fall
items.summary: Storm Hunter is a step closer to her first French Open main draw appearance since 2023, as Priscilla Hon and Maddison Inglis fall short in the qualifiers.

items.title: Researchers uncover longest whale journey ever documented with fluke photos
items.summary: Scientists analysing a database of photos find two humpback whales travelled from Queensland to Brazil, covering a record distance of 14,200km.

items.title: Arsenal celebrate winning first Premier League title in 22 years
items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Fisheries body pushes for carp virus trial in Victoria's Wimmera as frustrations grow over delays
items.summary: The Victorian Fisheries Authority wants to host a carp virus trial, but the federal government says it needs regulatory approval, which will delay research.

items.title: ASX falls below 8,500 level as rate rise fears surface — as it happened
items.summary: The Australian share market has fallen below 8,500 points after a US bond sell-off triggered further losses on Wall Street. Oil prices slipped after Donald Trump said he had been an hour away from ordering an attack on Iran before postponing it.

items.title: Earthquake in outback SA has 'permanently changed landscape'
items.summary: Cracks in the ground in outback Australia caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake could help researchers predict future seismic activity.

items.title: Southampton expelled from Premier League playoffs for spying on rivals
items.summary: Southampton loses its chance to gain promotion to the Premier League after being caught spying on Middlesbrough and two other rivals.

items.title: How crepuscular rays are creating 'beautiful' sunsets across the Top End
items.summary: A "beautiful" optical illusion has been impacting sunsets in Australia's north, with a little-known weather phenomenon appearing to split golden hour into different coloured beams.

items.title: Chalmers returns serve to NSW premier over tax comments — as it happened
items.summary: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has rejected NSW Premier Chris Minns's comments on bracket creep.

items.title: Will double-strength bait stop Western and South Australia's mouse plague?
items.summary: Mice in their millions are eating grain crops, destroying food, and invading houses. Here's how it all started.

items.title: Former senior NT child protection staffers speak out amid sector's condemnation of legislative changes
items.summary: In the three weeks since the alleged murder of five-year-old Kumanjayi Little Baby in Alice Springs, the NT's child protection system has come under intense scrutiny. Now, former child protection workers are speaking out about why they believe the system is broken.

items.title: Arsenal wins Premier League after 22-year drought following Manchester City's draw against Bournemouth
items.summary: Arsenal's long wait for a Premier League title comes to an end as title challengers Manchester City drop points at Bournemouth.

items.title: UK police investigating child sex abuse allegations relating to Epstein files
items.summary: A British police force has said it is investigating two allegations of child sex abuse following information in documents relating to the late sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

items.title: After booting Hawthorn out of Tasmania, the Devils must deliver for Launceston
items.summary: They may not be scheduled to play their first AFL game until 2028, but the Tasmania Devils have claimed their first scalp — booting Hawthorn out of the state which has been its second home for more than two decades.

items.title: Formal complaint from ex-boss led to investigation into The Hobart Clinic's corporate governance
items.summary: Tasmania's largest private mental health hospital, which was given a $2 million lifeline by the state government last year, had its corporate governance placed under review, following a formal complaint by the facility's former chief executive.

items.title: Can alternative content help to fight the influence of the 'manosphere'?
items.summary: "Manosphere" rhetoric is getting louder online, but can men showcasing different lifestyles and values online cut through that noise?

items.title: Contentious developments receiving fast-tracked approval under affordable housing pathway
items.summary: Property developers are using an affordable housing scheme to bypass normal planning requirements and win fast-tracked approval for contentious projects across Melbourne.

items.title: Small businesses and startups go viral with AI Albo but tax experts 'rubbish' CGT claims
items.summary: One startup founder who helped spread viral memes about capital gains tax reforms says the campaign was aimed at attracting attention after the budget, and not necessarily focused on accuracy.

items.title: Vanuatu agrees to pacts with China and Australia
items.summary: Jotham Napat has confirmed Vanuatu has agreed to two pacts with both countries, but is not happy with either party.

items.title: Life on the islands of the Strait of Hormuz during the Iran war
items.summary: Before rising conflict pushed the Strait of Hormuz into the spotlight as a choke point vital to economies globally, tourism transformed women's lives.

items.title: Labor walks tightrope as tax overhaul alarms startups and investors
items.summary: Labor MPs are gearing up for a hard sell on the impact of the federal government's tax changes on some small businesses, trusts and shares, despite many reporting "positive" feedback on housing reforms.

items.title: Angine de Poitrine, the French Canadian costumed microtonal duo breaking the internet
items.summary: French Canadian duo Angine de Poitrine are riding a wave of interest after a video of their live performance exploded in popularity, aided by a comical reaction by many on YouTube struggling to understand how their music is made.

items.title: Travel quiz, May 20: Got the Wednesday blues? Let's take a trip around the world
items.summary: Can you name the city in this picture? And what is the Canadian specialty food "beaver tail"?

items.title: Government and Opposition clash over budget announcements
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Musk v Altman – the clash of the tech titans
items.summary: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

items.title: Inquest hears elderly man who died was given another person's prescription medication
items.summary: An 82-year-old South Australian man died after he ingested prescription medication that was meant for another person and had been incorrectly labelled, a coronial inquest is told.

items.title: Undersea volcano erupts in Papua New Guinea's Bismarck Sea prompting tsunami concerns
items.summary: The Titan Ridge Volcano is erupting in the Bismarck Sea, sending ash and smoke into the sky and prompting authorities to warn of possible tsunamis in the area.

items.title: Massage therapist Cameron Petrie pleads guilty to raping Sunshine Coast mother
items.summary: A Queensland massage therapist has been imprisoned for raping a client who says "silence is not consent" and that the "degrading" experience devastated her emotionally, physically and financially.

items.title: Brisbane Heat back Cricket Australia plan to open BBL season in India
items.summary: Cricket Australia has spoken with BBL franchises about a plan to open this season's BBL with a showdown in India.

items.title: Bureau of Meteorology executive Peter Stone, who oversaw bungled website revamp, departs role
items.summary: Peter Stone was acting CEO when the BOM launched its new website, which was widely criticised for its costs and poor design.

items.title: Reserve Bank worries about inflation pressures building, risk of a recession
items.summary: The Reserve Bank is growing increasingly anxious about price pressures building in the economy and its chief economist has raised the risk of a recession in post-budget remarks.

items.title: Economist blames ACT's fiscal deterioration entirely on government's 'conscious policy decisions'
items.summary: An independent economist warns Canberrans will need to pay higher taxes and charges if the ACT government continues the same amount of spending on infrastructure and services.

items.title: TISM fined for $18,000 in damage to Sydney Opera House ahead of tour
items.summary: The anarchic, ARIA-winning group has been hit with a hefty fine by the Sydney Opera House before its first national tour in more than 30 years.

items.title: Why Queensland rookie Sam Walker is the great unknown of State of Origin I
items.summary: Sam Walker has always seemed like a player destined for the highest levels of rugby league, but ahead of his State of Origin debut, it's impossible to know what comes next for the new Queensland halfback.

items.title: Two people die from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking NT health alert
items.summary: Two people have died from Murray Valley encephalitis in Alice Springs, sparking a health alert for the rare mosquito-borne disease across the Northern Territory.

items.title: Eligibility expanded for Queensland's free nasal spray flu vaccine program
items.summary: With stocks set to expire in August, the FluMist nasal spray has been made free for all children over the age of two.

items.title: Key takeaways from Sam Altman and Elon Musk's bitter legal battle over OpenAI
items.summary: A legal showdown between two of the tech world's most powerful men has reached its end. Here's what we learned throughout the trial.

items.title: Office of the Director of Public Prosecutions gets $18.9 million in funding after sounding alarm over rising caseload
items.summary: The funding will go towards 24 new, permanent positions within the office to meet its increasing case load, Attorney-General Tara Cheyne said.

items.title: Network 10 to go off air in country SA and NSW as WIN Television deal lapses
items.summary: The end of a broadcast deal between Network 10 and Win Television is set to leave three regional markets without access to multiple channels.

items.title: SA Power Networks' $10 million plan to combat insulator pollution linked to regional summer power outages
items.summary: SA Power Networks is investing about $10 million to combat insulator pollution, which has been linked to repeated power outages across regional South Australia over summer.

items.title: Scientists investigate mystery illness paralysing and killing hundreds of magpies
items.summary: Researchers say they are still far from identifying the cause of a deadly paralytic illness spreading through populations of the iconic Australian birds.

items.title: Tasmania government apologises for display of autopsy body parts in museum without consent
items.summary: A formal apology is given in Tasmania's parliament for the past practice of taking human specimens from autopsies and displaying them in a museum without the knowledge of family members.

items.title: 'Nowhere for me to exist': Growing up as a biracial child in Australia
items.summary: KA was "stripped" of her culture as a child and has been on a journey to reclaim her connection to her Filipina heritage.

items.title: Hawthorn 'extremely disappointed' after AFL forces club to end Tasmanian partnership
items.summary: Hawthorn is less than pleased after being forced to cease playing home games in Tasmania ahead of the Devils' AFL entry.

items.title: Neymar included in Brazil's FIFA World Cup squad despite three-year international hiatus
items.summary: Carlo Ancelotti includes Brazil's all-time leading goalscorer in his first FIFA World Cup squad.

items.title: Shelter WA calls for targeted Airbnb ban as report reveals long-term rentals outnumbered 15–1
items.summary: There are renewed calls for a ban on short-stay rentals as a new report shows holiday homes vastly outnumber permanent rentals in small regional communities.

items.title: Northern Territory diphtheria outbreak spreads across borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia
items.summary: An ongoing diphtheria outbreak in the Northern Territory has now spread across its borders to Western Australia, Queensland and South Australia, with Health Minister Mark Butler describing it as the nation's worst "for decades".

items.summary: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

items.title: Long-time Nationals MP Tim Bull decides not to contest 2026 Victorian election
items.summary: Tim Bull has announced he will not contest the November election, after representing Gippsland East for the National Party for 16 years.

items.title: Arsenal edges closer to Premier League title after nervy win as VAR spares Kai Havertz red card
items.summary: Kai Havertz emerges Arsenal's hero after scoring the winner against Burnley, and is spared a sending-off for a horror tackle.

items.title: Historic flour mill walls demolished despite grassroots fight to save Mount Gambier structure
items.summary: In the face of protests, the remnants of a historic flour mill in Mount Gambier are now a pile of crumbled stone. The site will be developed into a multi-storey hotel complex and townhouses.

items.title: UK's Channel 4 removes all Married at First Sight episodes after BBC reports sexual assault allegations
items.summary: British broadcaster Channel 4 removes all previous seasons of Married at First Sight after rape allegations come to light.

items.title: Bernard Tomic edges closer to shock French Open return as fellow Australians struggle in qualifiers
items.summary: Bernard Tomic is just two wins from reaching his first main draw of the French Open since 2019 after being the only Australian to enjoy success in the qualifiers.

items.title: ASX jumps more than 1 per cent in rebound rally — as it happened
items.summary: The local share market rebounded Monday's heavy selling, as shares in Tuas made back some of their heavy losses. Look back on the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News markets blog.

items.title: Collins-class submarine overhaul scaled back in 'pivot to reduce risk' — as it happened
items.summary: Changes are being made to massive program aiming to upgrade and extend the life of Australia's aging fleet of Collins Class submarines, Defence Minister Richard Marles has confirmed.
